drafts.text:
PR descriptions. The part of the workflow everyone half-asses.

Press ⌥ Space, say what changed and why — 30 seconds, full context for reviewers, lands wherever your cursor is. No window-switching. No typing.

Jot is free, open-source Mac dictation: hotkey, speak, text at cursor. On-device on the Neural Engine. No cloud, no subscription.

What part of your dev workflow are you still typing when you could speak it?

#indiedev #macOS #buildinpublic

drafts.note: Attach pr-description-tip.svg (export as PNG). No link in post body — post the utm_url as the first reply immediately after posting. ~168 characters, within 200-char limit.

drafts.text:
I started dictating my PR descriptions instead of typing them — here's what changed

For months, my PR descriptions looked like this: 'Fixed the thing. Tests pass.' I knew they were bad. But typing a proper description after writing the code felt like the worst overhead — you just want to push.

Then I started using Jot (free, open-source Mac dictation I built). Press ⌥ Space in my terminal or browser, speak what I changed and why, it pastes into the PR description field. The whole thing takes 25–30 seconds.

What changed: my PR descriptions now actually explain context. What changed, why, what to look for in the review. Same information I'd tell a teammate verbally — just transcribed.

The thing I didn't expect: speaking is more natural than typing for explanations. When I type, I compress. When I speak, I give context. Reviewers have noticed.

A few things I found:
- Jot pastes wherever your cursor is, so no window-switching
- On-device transcription means it works offline; nothing leaves your Mac
- ~150-word limit fits comfortably in a solid PR description

Apple Silicon only, macOS 14+. Free and open source.

(I built this — disclosure)

Link in comments.

notes: v1.14.1 confirmed at 75 downloads via GitHub MCP (was ~33 on June 19); total estimated at 194 (+42 from June 21 baseline of 152, avg7=10/day). The acceleration is real — 42 new downloads in 3 days suggests Show HN residual + sustained organic discovery. Two new free competitors appearing in roundups today: Spokenly (free local Parakeet) and Voibe ($149 lifetime) — Jot is absent from all coverage including their own comparison articles. iTunes API 403 (8th+ consecutive day); appstore.json date updated to 2026-06-22, all other values preserved including installs:0 from App Store Connect. Outreach window reopens June 26 — priority targets: machow2.com and speakmac.app (both run high-SEO Mac dictation roundups that exclude Jot). Reddit recommended:false (last true June 19; earliest June 23). Instagram recommended:false (last true June 20; earliest June 23). Monday is the strongest LinkedIn day — PR description angle targets the developer cohort from Show HN.
